CAT15-007.headline: Alone In A Crowd
CAT15-007.subtitle: Social isolation versus perceived loneliness
CAT15-007.figureLine: A single clay figure stands amid several other clay figures yet faces slightly away with shoulders drawn inward, posture quietly apart, conveying feeling unseen among others.
CAT15-007.sceneLine: A faint plum-accent thread reaches from the figure toward the surrounding forms but never quite connects, drifting in soft light plum, suggesting closeness in space without true contact.

CAT15-008.headline: Loneliness And The Body
CAT15-008.subtitle: How loneliness weakens immune and physical health
CAT15-008.figureLine: A single clay figure sits with arms wrapped around its own torso, posture protective and slightly depleted, conveying the quiet physical toll of being unseen.
CAT15-008.sceneLine: A soft light plum glow that once surrounded the figure thins and recedes at its edges into pale plum, suggesting warmth and resilience slowly drawn down by isolation.

CAT15-009.headline: The Roots Of Loneliness
CAT15-009.subtitle: Evolutionary origins of social pain
CAT15-009.figureLine: A single clay figure stands at the edge of an open space, head turned back toward where others would be, posture instinctively pulled toward the group.
CAT15-009.sceneLine: A faint plum-accent signal pulses softly outward from the figure like a call across distance, fading into pale plum, suggesting an ancient alarm that loneliness still triggers.

CAT15-010.headline: Measuring Loneliness
CAT15-010.subtitle: Understanding the UCLA Loneliness Scale
CAT15-010.figureLine: A single clay figure stands holding one small rounded form up before it, posture considered and reflective, conveying a careful look at one's own felt distance from others.
CAT15-010.sceneLine: A gentle gradient of plum tones ranges beside the figure from soft light plum to pale plum, suggesting loneliness understood as a quiet spectrum rather than a fixed state.

CAT15-013.headline: Friendships In Your Twenties
CAT15-013.subtitle: Why friendships get harder with age
CAT15-013.figureLine: A single clay figure stretches one hand toward distant forms that have drifted apart, posture reaching but unhurried, conveying the growing effort connection takes.
CAT15-013.sceneLine: Several plum-accent strands that once met now stretch thin and far apart, holding their soft light plum color, suggesting bonds that take more reach to keep across busier lives.

CAT15-015.headline: Midlife Loneliness
CAT15-015.subtitle: How social circles quietly erode over years
CAT15-015.figureLine: A single clay figure stands within a wide, sparse ring, posture steady but surrounded by empty space, conveying a circle that has thinned slowly over time.
CAT15-015.sceneLine: A ring of plum-accent forms around the figure shows gaps where pieces have quietly fallen away, the remaining shapes glowing pale plum, suggesting a social circle eroding unnoticed.

CAT15-019.headline: The Isolated Caregiver
CAT15-019.subtitle: Loneliness among family caregivers
CAT15-019.figureLine: A single clay figure leans toward a smaller form it tends with both hands, posture devoted yet turned away from the world behind it, conveying care that quietly narrows one's own circle.
CAT15-019.sceneLine: A soft light plum thread connects the figure to the one it cares for while other plum-accent threads behind it grow faint, suggesting connection given fully in one direction.

CAT15-020.headline: The Search For Belonging
CAT15-020.subtitle: LGBTQ+ loneliness and minority stress
CAT15-020.figureLine: A single clay figure walks forward with quiet determination, head lifted and searching, posture hopeful despite a long path, conveying the steady seeking of a place to belong.
CAT15-020.sceneLine: A faint plum-accent glow appears ahead of the figure like a gathering warmth on the horizon, deepening from pale plum toward soft light plum, suggesting belonging found further along the way.

CAT15-021.headline: Making Friends As An Adult
CAT15-021.subtitle: How to make friends according to research
CAT15-021.figureLine: A single clay figure extends one open hand toward another nearby form, posture warm and slightly tentative, conveying the first deliberate move toward a new connection.
CAT15-021.sceneLine: A new plum-accent thread begins to form in the space between the figure and another, glowing soft light plum where they almost meet, suggesting a friendship just starting to take shape.

CAT15-023.headline: Deepening A Friendship
CAT15-023.subtitle: Moving from acquaintance to close friend
CAT15-023.figureLine: A single clay figure leans in close toward another form, posture relaxed and trusting, conveying the gradual softening from polite distance into genuine closeness.
CAT15-023.sceneLine: A thin plum-accent thread between the figure and another grows thicker and warmer, brightening from pale plum to soft light plum, suggesting acquaintance slowly deepening into closeness.

CAT15-024.headline: Friendship Skills
CAT15-024.subtitle: Habits that strengthen relationships
CAT15-024.figureLine: A single clay figure sits attentively with both hands cupped open in a gesture of listening, posture present and generous, conveying the everyday skills that hold friendships together.
CAT15-024.sceneLine: Several plum-accent strands beside the figure are being gently tended and woven tighter, glowing soft light plum, suggesting connection strengthened through small, repeated care.

CAT15-025.headline: Repairing A Friendship
CAT15-025.subtitle: How to mend a bond after conflict
CAT15-025.figureLine: A single clay figure reaches carefully toward a slightly turned-away form, posture humble and willing, conveying the tender first step back after a rupture.
CAT15-025.sceneLine: A plum-accent thread that had frayed is being drawn back together at its broken point, mending into soft light plum, suggesting a friendship carefully repaired after conflict.

CAT15-026.headline: Building A Friend Group
CAT15-026.subtitle: How to find and build close friends
CAT15-026.figureLine: A single clay figure stands at the center of several gathering forms, posture welcoming with arms slightly open, conveying the quiet work of drawing people together.
CAT15-026.sceneLine: Several plum-accent threads converge toward the figure from different directions, meeting in a warm soft light plum cluster, suggesting separate connections weaving into one close group.

CAT15-027.headline: Making The First Move
CAT15-027.subtitle: Overcoming the fear of reaching out
CAT15-027.figureLine: A single clay figure lifts one hand mid-gesture toward a distant form, posture caught between hesitation and courage, conveying the brave moment of reaching out first.
CAT15-027.sceneLine: A single plum-accent thread tentatively crosses an open gap toward another form, glowing brighter in soft light plum as it goes, suggesting fear giving way to the first contact.

CAT15-028.headline: The Power Of Vulnerability
CAT15-028.subtitle: How opening up builds closer friendships
CAT15-028.figureLine: A single clay figure lowers its protective arms and turns openly toward another form, posture exposed yet steady, conveying the courage of letting oneself be truly seen.
CAT15-028.sceneLine: A soft inner glow within the figure is revealed and reaches outward in soft light plum toward another, suggesting that opening up lets warmth pass between people.

CAT15-029.headline: Long-Distance Friendships
CAT15-029.subtitle: Strategies that actually keep them strong
CAT15-029.figureLine: A single clay figure stands holding firmly to one end of a long thread that stretches far into the distance, posture committed and patient, conveying connection maintained across miles.
CAT15-029.sceneLine: A plum-accent thread stretches across a wide dark gap yet stays unbroken and glowing soft light plum at both ends, suggesting a bond held steady over great distance.

CAT15-030.headline: When A Friendship Ends
CAT15-030.subtitle: Recognizing a friendship has run its course
CAT15-030.figureLine: A single clay figure stands quietly as one thread loosens from its hand, posture accepting rather than grasping, conveying a gentle, peaceful letting go.
CAT15-030.sceneLine: A plum-accent thread softly comes untied at one end and drifts away, fading from soft light plum into pale plum, suggesting a connection gracefully reaching its natural close.

CAT15-031.headline: The Psychology Of Belonging
CAT15-031.subtitle: Why belonging matters for mental health
CAT15-031.figureLine: A single clay figure settles comfortably into a circle of surrounding forms, posture at ease and rooted, conveying the deep steadiness of feeling one truly belongs.
CAT15-031.sceneLine: A warm plum-accent glow encircles the figure and the forms around it as one continuous ring, holding soft light plum, suggesting the quiet security of belonging.

CAT15-032.headline: Volunteering And The Brain
CAT15-032.subtitle: How giving reduces loneliness
CAT15-032.figureLine: A single clay figure bends to offer a small rounded form to another nearby figure, posture giving and warmed by the act, conveying connection found through helping others.
CAT15-032.sceneLine: A plum-accent thread lights up between the figure and the one it helps, the glow returning to warm the giver in soft light plum, suggesting that giving rekindles connection.

CAT15-033.headline: Community Through Shared Interests
CAT15-033.subtitle: Finding belonging in common values
CAT15-033.figureLine: A single clay figure stands among forms all leaning toward one shared point, posture aligned and engaged, conveying the ease of connecting over something held in common.
CAT15-033.sceneLine: Several plum-accent threads from different forms gather around one shared glowing center in soft light plum, suggesting community built on common ground.

CAT15-034.headline: Faith And Connection
CAT15-034.subtitle: How religious communities support mental health
CAT15-034.figureLine: A single clay figure stands gathered with others in a calm, attentive cluster, posture quiet and held, conveying the steadiness of shared belonging in community.
CAT15-034.sceneLine: A gentle plum-accent glow rises softly above the gathered forms and settles over them like shared warmth in pale plum, suggesting comfort drawn from communal connection.

CAT15-036.headline: The Power Of Third Places
CAT15-036.subtitle: Why community spaces matter for mental health
CAT15-036.figureLine: A single clay figure rests easily within an open gathering area, posture unhurried and welcome, conveying the comfort of a shared space that belongs to everyone.
CAT15-036.sceneLine: A warm plum-accent area on the ground holds several forms loosely together in soft light plum, suggesting a third place where people gently come together.

CAT15-037.headline: Purpose Against Loneliness
CAT15-037.subtitle: How meaning protects against isolation
CAT15-037.figureLine: A single clay figure stands oriented toward a steady point ahead, posture purposeful and upright, conveying the grounding pull of a meaningful direction.
CAT15-037.sceneLine: A clear plum-accent light glows steadily ahead of the figure, drawing a warm path in soft light plum, suggesting purpose as a guard against drifting into loneliness.

CAT15-038.headline: Activity-Based Groups
CAT15-038.subtitle: Low-barrier ways to build connection
CAT15-038.figureLine: A single clay figure works alongside several others on a shared simple task, posture engaged and side by side, conveying connection formed naturally through doing something together.
CAT15-038.sceneLine: Several plum-accent threads form quietly between the figures as they share one activity, glowing soft light plum, suggesting bonds built easily through shared doing.

CAT15-039.headline: Online Communities
CAT15-039.subtitle: When virtual belonging is real
CAT15-039.figureLine: A single clay figure leans toward distant forms connected only by long threads, posture engaged and genuinely warmed, conveying real closeness held across distance.
CAT15-039.sceneLine: Plum-accent threads span a wide gap to link the figure with forms far away, glowing as warmly in soft light plum as if near, suggesting virtual belonging that is truly felt.

CAT15-042.headline: Online Friendships
CAT15-042.subtitle: Do digital connections offer real support
CAT15-042.figureLine: A single clay figure rests one hand on a thread that leads off into the distance, posture trusting and supported, conveying genuine comfort drawn from a faraway friend.
CAT15-042.sceneLine: A plum-accent thread reaches across distance to gently steady the figure from afar, glowing soft light plum where it touches, suggesting real support flowing through digital bonds.

CAT15-044.headline: Chronic Illness And Loneliness
CAT15-044.subtitle: How chronic illness creates social isolation
CAT15-044.figureLine: A single clay figure sits calmly within a softly enclosed quiet space, posture resting and gently held, conveying steadiness even when the wider world feels distant.
CAT15-044.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent boundary surrounds the figure while faint pale plum threads still reach gently inward from beyond it, suggesting connection that endures across the distance illness can create.

CAT15-045.headline: The Loneliness Crisis
CAT15-045.subtitle: How the pandemic revealed widespread isolation
CAT15-045.figureLine: Several clay figures stand spaced far apart across an open ground, each posture quietly turned toward the others, conveying many people feeling alone at the very same time.
CAT15-045.sceneLine: Faint plum-accent threads stretch thin across the wide gaps between the scattered figures, holding pale plum, suggesting a shared loneliness quietly connecting many at once.

CAT17-025.headline: Caregiver Guilt
CAT17-025.subtitle: Holding boundaries without sacrificing yourself
CAT17-025.figureLine: A single clay figure stands balanced between tending a smaller form and turning gently toward its own space, posture caring yet steady, conveying compassion held with healthy limits.
CAT17-025.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent line traces a calm, clear boundary around the figure while a warm thread still reaches toward the one it cares for in soft light plum, suggesting care kept whole through boundaries.

CAT17-031.headline: Loneliness In Older Adults
CAT17-031.subtitle: Health consequences and warning signs
CAT17-031.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests with hands folded quietly in a still space, posture settled but somewhat withdrawn, conveying the quiet isolation that can settle in later years.
CAT17-031.sceneLine: A faint plum-accent glow surrounds the figure with its outer threads grown thin, holding pale plum, suggesting a circle that has quietly narrowed and longs to widen again.

CAT17-032.headline: Isolation Versus Loneliness
CAT17-032.subtitle: Why the two are not the same thing
CAT17-032.figureLine: A single clay figure stands alone in open space yet with a calm, untroubled posture, conveying solitude that is not the same as the ache of feeling alone.
CAT17-032.sceneLine: Two distinct plum-accent forms sit side by side, one a hollow ring and one a glowing core, both in soft light plum, suggesting being apart and feeling alone as two different things.

CAT17-033.headline: Video Calls And Connection
CAT17-033.subtitle: How video calls reduce loneliness in older adults
CAT17-033.figureLine: A single seated clay figure leans warmly toward a glowing form before it, posture brightened and engaged, conveying genuine closeness reached across a screen.
CAT17-033.sceneLine: A warm plum-accent glow rises before the seated figure and reaches gently toward its face area in soft light plum, suggesting a faraway loved one made present through a call.

CAT17-036.headline: The Comfort Of Pets
CAT17-036.subtitle: How pet ownership reduces loneliness
CAT17-036.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests one hand on a small companion form nestled at its side, posture softened and comforted, conveying steady wordless companionship.
CAT17-036.sceneLine: A gentle plum-accent glow gathers warmly between the figure and the small form beside it in soft light plum, suggesting comfort and connection drawn from a faithful companion.

CAT17-037.headline: Intergenerational Connection
CAT17-037.subtitle: How old and young benefit together
CAT17-037.figureLine: A taller and a smaller clay figure lean toward one another in easy exchange, postures mutually attentive, conveying warmth flowing both ways across generations.
CAT17-037.sceneLine: A plum-accent thread loops warmly between the two figures of different size, glowing equally bright in soft light plum at each end, suggesting mutual benefit shared across ages.

CAT17-042.headline: Faith Communities And Aging
CAT17-042.subtitle: How faith reduces loneliness in older adults
CAT17-042.figureLine: A single older clay figure stands gently held within a small gathering of forms, posture calm and accompanied, conveying belonging that endures into later life.
CAT17-042.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent warmth wraps around the figure and the forms beside it as one gentle band in pale plum, suggesting steady companionship found in faith community.

CAT19-006.headline: Male Loneliness
CAT19-006.subtitle: The adult friendship crisis among men
CAT19-006.figureLine: A single clay figure stands solidly but apart, arms held close and gaze toward distant forms, posture guarded yet quietly longing, conveying isolation behind a steady front.
CAT19-006.sceneLine: A faint plum-accent thread reaches from the figure toward distant forms but stays thin and tentative, holding pale plum, suggesting connection wanted but rarely reached for.

CAT19-016.headline: Shallow Male Friendships
CAT19-016.subtitle: Why men's friendships lack emotional depth
CAT19-016.figureLine: A single clay figure stands beside another at a polite, fixed distance, posture friendly but unyielding, conveying closeness that stays on the surface.
CAT19-016.sceneLine: A plum-accent thread links the figure to another but stays thin and taut, glowing only faintly in pale plum, suggesting a bond present yet never allowed to deepen.

CAT19-017.headline: Male Family Caregivers
CAT19-017.subtitle: An invisible and underserved population
CAT19-017.figureLine: A single clay figure tends a smaller form with quiet steadiness while standing slightly in shadow, posture devoted yet overlooked, conveying care given without being seen.
CAT19-017.sceneLine: A warm plum-accent thread runs from the figure to the one it cares for while a faint pale plum light only just begins to fall on the caregiver, suggesting care that deserves to be noticed.

CAT19-018.headline: When Vulnerability Feels Dangerous
CAT19-018.subtitle: Why opening up feels risky for men
CAT19-018.figureLine: A single clay figure holds one arm protectively across itself while the other begins to lower, posture caught between guarding and opening, conveying the fear of being seen.
CAT19-018.sceneLine: A guarded plum-accent shell around the figure shows one small opening where soft light plum warmth begins to slip through, suggesting vulnerability cautiously becoming possible.

CAT19-019.headline: Father And Son
CAT19-019.subtitle: How the bond shapes emotional health in men
CAT19-019.figureLine: A taller and a younger clay figure stand close with one steadying hand passing between them, postures linked, conveying emotional patterns handed down through the bond.
CAT19-019.sceneLine: A plum-accent thread passes from the taller form into the younger one and continues onward, glowing soft light plum, suggesting an emotional blueprint shaped across generations.

CAT19-046.headline: Men And Domestic Abuse
CAT19-046.subtitle: Recognizing and supporting male victims
CAT19-046.figureLine: A single clay figure sits quietly within a calm, sheltered space with one hand resting on steady ground, posture settling toward safety, conveying steadiness gently returning.
CAT19-046.sceneLine: A soft plum-accent light gathers protectively around the figure like a calm shelter in pale plum, suggesting safety and care held close, never the harm itself.

CAT19-047.headline: Men's Sheds
CAT19-047.subtitle: How community workshops support mental health
CAT19-047.figureLine: Several clay figures work side by side at a shared low surface, postures occupied and companionable, conveying connection built shoulder to shoulder over honest work.
CAT19-047.sceneLine: Quiet plum-accent threads form between the working figures as they share one task, glowing soft light plum, suggesting friendship grown gently through making things together.

CAT19-054.headline: Men's Support Groups
CAT19-054.subtitle: Answering the male loneliness epidemic
CAT19-054.figureLine: Several clay figures sit in a small open circle leaning slightly inward toward one another, postures attentive and unguarded, conveying men beginning to truly open up together.
CAT19-054.sceneLine: Plum-accent threads cross the open center of the circle to link the seated figures, brightening into soft light plum, suggesting isolation easing through shared support.

CAT19-056.headline: Male Mentorship
CAT19-056.subtitle: Rewriting emotional blueprints for mental health
CAT19-056.figureLine: An older clay figure walks beside a younger one with a guiding hand near its shoulder, postures aligned in the same direction, conveying steadying guidance passed forward.
CAT19-056.sceneLine: A warm plum-accent thread runs from the older figure to the younger and redraws a clearer path ahead in soft light plum, suggesting an emotional blueprint being rewritten.

CAT19-060.headline: Cooking Programs For Men
CAT19-060.subtitle: How shared meals support men's mental health
CAT19-060.figureLine: Several clay figures gather around a shared low surface preparing something together, postures cooperative and at ease, conveying connection built around a common table.
CAT19-060.sceneLine: A warm plum-accent glow rises from the shared surface and draws the figures gently together in soft light plum, suggesting companionship nourished through cooking side by side.

CAT19-062.headline: Volunteering And Men
CAT19-062.subtitle: How giving supports mental health in men
CAT19-062.figureLine: A single clay figure leans forward to offer a steadying hand to another form, posture purposeful and warmed by the act, conveying wellbeing found through being useful to others.
CAT19-062.sceneLine: A plum-accent thread brightens between the figure and the one it helps and curls back to warm the giver in soft light plum, suggesting connection and meaning returned through service.

CAT20-046.headline: Partners In Chronic Illness
CAT20-046.subtitle: Mental health of those who care for a partner
CAT20-046.figureLine: A single clay figure stands beside a resting form with one supportive hand extended, posture steady and tender yet carrying its own quiet weight, conveying love that also bears strain.
CAT20-046.sceneLine: A warm plum-accent thread holds the two forms together while a soft pale plum light begins to settle on the supporting figure too, suggesting care that also needs care.

CAT20-067.headline: Online Health Communities
CAT20-067.subtitle: Benefits and risks for chronic illness
CAT20-067.figureLine: A single clay figure reaches toward distant forms linked by long threads, posture seeking understanding and gentle support, conveying connection found among others who understand.
CAT20-067.sceneLine: Plum-accent threads span the distance to link the figure with faraway forms, glowing warmly in soft light plum while a few thin strands waver, suggesting real support held with quiet care.
